audit: DYN-5 hidden-messenger dynamics at one loop

checks.name: lambda = sqrt(zeta): lambda^2 - zeta machine zero and tex digits match
|lambda|, |zeta|, arg zeta reproduce the tex anchors
companion caveat anchor |lambda|^2/(16 pi^2) = 8.259697e-4
higher-order remark |zeta|^2 ~ 1.7e-2 reproduced
K_tr^2 = I/3 and K_tr^-1 = 3 K_tr (fixed convention)
messenger mass matrix M_* K_tr^-1 has ALL singular values sqrt(3) M_* => exact family degeneracy => delta Z_N proportional to identity at leading log
portal coupling matrix is lambda * 1_3: (Y+ Y)/(16 pi^2) = per-efold coefficient times identity
strict Route-B benchmark interval ln(M_*/M_X) = -ln(3)/2 (X sits AT sqrt(3) M_*): |delta Z_N| = 4.537102e-4
archival closure card present with the DYN-4a provenance sha256
benchmark replay reproduces the zeta anchor (links S3 to DYN-4a)
EXACT light-sector silence: m_nu invariant under the canonical redefinition (universal AND non-universal delta Z_N) at machine level given cond(M_R) ~ 1.6e5
linearized replay residual scales as O(deltaZ^2) (halving deltaZ quarters the residual)
at the strict benchmark |deltaZ| = 4.53e-4 the linearized-formula residual sits BELOW even the refreshed window
heavy sector NOT silent: all M_R singular values shift by exactly (1 - deltaZ); worst scanned shift stays inside the DYN-4b M_1 window
normalized zeta extraction EXACTLY invariant under delta Z_N (only M_* rescales by 1 - deltaZ)
R enumeration: all base channels have R = 2; every X-decorated Dirac/triplet/Majorana operator has R = 2 + m (zero violations)
portal forces R(X) = 2 - R(16); silence fails ONLY at the measure-zero assignment R(16) = 2 (benchmark R(16) = 1 is safe)

SUSY non-renormalization => one-loop effects are Kahler-only; the nu^c leg appears ONLY in Y_nuD => delta Y_u = delta Y_d = delta Y_e = delta C_5L = delta C_5R = 0 at one loop; X, N visible singlets => Delta b = (0,0,0), DYN-2 threshold vector untouched
two-loop leakage (ORDER ESTIMATE): shift in alpha^-1 over the full run < 1e-3, i.e. far below the DYN-2 threshold-sensitivity scale O(0.1); two-loop silence itself is NOT claimed

spurion regeneration condition k r_s = -m: r_s = -1 regenerates m = 1 at first order; r_s = -2 (constant-W / gravitino type) regenerates ONLY even m (m = 2 at k = 1), never the m = 1 portal decoration; positive-R spurions never regenerate at the holomorphic level
spurion ceilings vs DYN-4 windows computed (ORDER estimates): loose window tolerates eps ~ 1e-2, refreshed window forces eps below ~ 1e-3

S1_anchors.note: the tex caveat number 8.259697e-4 is the PER-E-FOLD coefficient of delta Z_N; the full delta Z_N multiplies ln(M_*/M_X)

derivation: W_R/M_* = M_V(N,N)/2 + lambda X(N) - K_tr^-1(X,X)/2 (paper theorem)
portal in components: lambda X_a N_a => Yukawa matrix lambda * 1_3
one-loop Kahler correction on N: delta Z_N = (Y+ Y)/(16pi^2) ln(M_*/M_X_i) summed over messenger mass eigenstates i
X mass matrix = M_* K_tr^-1 = 3 M_* K_tr; K_tr = P/sqrt(3) with P orthogonal symmetric (P^2 = I) => |eigenvalues| all sqrt(3) M_*
identical log arguments => delta Z_N = deltaZ * 1_3 EXACTLY at leading log (not just approximately family-universal)
S2_structure.strict_benchmark_note: in the strict theorem all three messengers sit at sqrt(3) M_*, ABOVE the matching scale; the caveat's generic interval covers microscopic completions where the operator-generation scale exceeds the messenger mass

S3_replay.structural_silence: m_nu = -mD M_R^-1 mD^T is invariant under mD -> mD A, M_R -> A^T M_R A for ANY invertible A: the seesaw combination is a GL(3)-covariant of the heavy field being integrated out, so canonical-normalization corrections cancel IDENTICALLY in every light observable

linearized_formula_validity.reading: the paper's linearized replay formulas are sufficient for ln(M_*/M_X) below the quoted crossing; beyond it the exact A-form must be used (this sharpens the tex caveat 'small, but not automatically smaller than the narrow contact-sensitivity windows')

heavy_sector.note: M_R eigenvalues, M_1 (leptogenesis input) and M_* all rescale by (1 - deltaZ); zeta itself does not move

spurion_conditions_to_audited_order: holomorphic spurions with POSITIVE R charge never regenerate X-decorated operators in W (holomorphy)

even-R spurions (r_s = -2, constant-W/gravitino type) preserve the residual (-1)^R parity: all ODD decorations (including the m = 1 Dirac decoration X 16 16 H) stay absent to ALL orders in the spurion; the m = 2 decoration can appear at O(eps)
odd-R spurions (r_s = -1) regenerate the m = 1 decoration at O(eps) and are the dangerous case

closure_card.note: user-restored archival artifact, same sha256 as the DYN-4a ledger

provenance.tex_caveat: paper/gut_framework.tex, canonical-basis caveat (delta Z_N observation) + R-selection lemma + tree-level matching-silence theorem
